channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: ZestyAI Names David Friend CFO, John Ross SVP
item.description: (John Ross, SVP, Client Development; and David Friend, CFO, ZestyAI. Source: ZestyAI.) ZestyAI (San Francisco) has appointed David Friend CFO and John Ross SVP of Client Development as the company expands use of its AI platform across the property/casualty insurance industry. Friend and Ross join as ZestyAI says it has turned cash flow positive, more than doubled product usage, surpassed 200 regulatory approvals and added 26 carrier customers.

item.title: Mythos Myths: Good Guys Hold More Cybersecurity Cards, Insurer CEO Says
item.description: Related articles : Anthropic Touts AI Cybersecurity Project With Big Tech Partners ; Anthropic to Let Partners Share Mythos Cybersecurity Findings With Others ; Fears of Unfettered Hacking Spurred by Anthropic’s Mythos AI Model Overblown ; Anthropic Releases Mythos-Like Model Without Cyber Capabilities : Mythos Proportions Two days before the government action, Williamson delivered his answer to a question about insurer cyber risk management posed by Taoufik Gharib, director and lead analyst for S&P Global Ratings, during an “Executive Perspectives” session of the S&P conference. That’s how Jim Williamson, president and chief executive officer of insurer and reinsurer Everest Group, Ltd., prefaced his answer to a question about managing cybersecurity risks in the age of AI during the S&P Global Ratings 42nd Annual Insurance Conference last week. “On cyber risk, we are in the same arms race we were before AI,” Williamson believes, chiding the media for exaggerating the Mythos model story and the ability of bad guys to get into companies and exploit faults at a pace.

item.title: ‘We need to stay relevant’: IUA director highlights communication as key to closing protection gap
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Swiss Re CorSo eyes data centres, ART, Japan as key pillars for Asian specialty push
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Mangione Drops Psychiatric Defense Bid for New York Murder Case
item.description: Luigi Mangione’s lawyers withdrew a plan for a psychiatric defense at his upcoming state murder trial in the shooting death of a UnitedHealth Group Inc. In a letter to New York state Judge Gregory Carro on Thursday, the defense team said they will withdraw the psychiatric defense. Mangione is charged with second-degree murder in the case that captured national headlines both for Thompson’s killing and a manhunt that ended days later at a McDonald’s in Altoona, Pennsylvania.

item.title: Waymo Recalling 3,900 Robotaxis Over Risk of Entering Closed Construction Zones
item.description: Waymo updated vehicle software to avoid entering construction zones. Alphabet self-driving unit Waymo is recalling nearly 3,900 robotaxis in the U.S. The latest recall, filed with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), stems from more than a dozen incidents since early April in California and Arizona in which Waymo autonomous vehicles (AVs) did not recognize and drove past ramp closure signs into pre-planned freeway construction zones and freeway lanes with active construction work.

item.title: US Northeast Threatened by Severe Thunderstorms, Damaging Winds
item.description: More than 63 million Americans in a Northeast Corridor that spans Washington, through New York, to Boston are threatened by wild weather on Thursday, including severe thunderstorms and damaging winds. The Northeast is in the firing line after Tropical Storm Arthur—the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic season—brought “life-threatening flooding” to the southeastern U.S. Arthur has degenerated into a low-pressure area along the upper Texas coast, but there’s still a chance of gusty winds, hail and possibly even a tornado across the Gulf Coast.

item.title: ‘Park Outside’ Fire Risk Recalls Jump Nearly 50%, Affecting 3.2M Vehicles Nationwide
item.description: The total number of Park Outside recalls jumped nearly 50% in the last week, to more than 3.2 million vehicles nationwide, according to new analysis by vehicle history provider CARFAX. “Although we never want to see a jump in these serious recalls, the good news is recall repairs are free.” Stellantis added in a statement that “affected customers will be notified” and that they anticipate “a solution no later than July.” The top 10 states with the most vehicles under Park Outside recalls are: Drivers can visit carfax.com/recall to see whether their vehicles have an open recall. Stellantis, the parent company of Chrysler and other brands, said the recalled vehicles, primarily affecting model-year 2021-2025 Jeep Wrangler SUVs and Gladiator pickup trucks, face a potential fire risk.

item.title: Most American Workers Are Checked Out, and Like ‘The Office,’ Their Bosses Are the Last to Know
item.description: One reason why most workers aren’t engaged on the job has to do with their psychological safety , meaning whether they feel they can speak up, ask questions or admit mistakes without being punished. Michael Scott, the hapless regional manager at the center of the American version of “The Office” played by Steve Carell, believed he was the world’s best boss . The joke worked because so many viewers recognized something universal: the gap between how bosses see themselves and how workers actually experience them.

item.title: The Skills People Still Perform Better Than AI
item.description: “And we think, especially now, in this time of AI advancement, that it’s the durable skills that really make a worker genuinely valuable at work, regardless of what tools and technology are available.” Here are five skills to cultivate based on the areas where experts say humans still hold an edge over artificial intelligence. Interpreting body language and reading between the lines to decipher what wasn’t explicitly communicated are skills that many people find are best performed by humans. But what if people have qualities both unmistakably human and essential to career success that AI could not easily replace them ?

item.title: FSB holds a roundtable on audit quality and structural shifts in the global audit industry
item.description: On 18 June 2026, the Financial Stability Board (FSB) convened a roundtable to explore the potential financial stability implications of structural shifts in the global audit industry. The roundtable aimed to enhance understanding of how these developments may impact audit quality, a critical factor in safeguarding financial stability. Participants included senior representatives from FSB member authorities and standard-setting bodies; audit oversight bodies; the International Federation of Accountants (IFAC); the International Forum of Independent Audit Regulators (IFIAR); the Committee of European Auditing Oversight Bodies (CEAOB); the international standard-setting bodies for auditing, assurance, and ethics in accountancy and their oversight body, the Public Interest Oversight Board (PIOB); the six largest global audit networks and a local audit firm, as well as other key stakeholders.

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: World-Model Startup Odyssey Leads With $310M In Slower Week For Large Deals
item.description: This week was not an exceptionally busy one for large funding deals, though we saw sizable rounds in a lively mix of sectors ranging from AI to fintech to quantum computing and cybersecurity. The biggest raise was for AI world-model developer Odyssey , which secured a $310 million Series B. Want to keep track of the largest startup funding deals in 2026 with our curated list of $100 million-plus venture deals to U.S.-based companies?

item.title: US severe convective storm (SCS) insured losses surpass $22bn in 2026 so far: Gallagher Re
item.description: Discussing SCS activity in the US and Canada through June 2026 so far, Gallagher Re’s experts explain, “The first two weeks of June brought nearly daily outbreaks of severe convective storm (SCS) activity that affected several major metro areas from the Rockies to the Northeast in the United States. Insurance industry losses from severe convective storms in the United States have now topped $22 billion as of June 18th, according to Gallagher Re. Recent severe convective storms (SCS) convective storm activity has driven additional billions of insurance market losses, the reinsurance broker explained in a new commentary.

item.title: HDI Global US appoints Jill Salmon as Head of Cyber Underwriting
item.description: HDI Global (HDI), a Corporate and Specialty Lines insurer, has announced the appointment of Jill Salmon as Head of Cyber Underwriting at HDI Global US. “Her deep expertise in the cyber space, proven leadership, and strong market presence will further enhance our capabilities as we continue to focus on underwriting excellence and disciplined growth in the US market while acting as our clients’ and brokers’ preferred partner in transformation.” Salmon commented, “I am thrilled to join HDI Global and excited to lead the continued development of the cyber business. I look forward to strengthening our market presence, deepening relationships across the market, and working with the team to establish a leading cyber platform.” Meike Raddatz, Head of Cyber at HDI Global, added, “Jill’s appointment marks an important step in the continued growth of our Cyber business in the US market.

item.title: Onchain protocol Re secures strategic investment from Coinbase Ventures
item.description: Re, the onchain protocol connecting real-world reinsurance capital with decentralised finance, has secured a strategic investment from Coinbase Ventures, the venture arm of Coinbase, to accelerate its mission of bringing reinsurance capital onchain and opening one of the world’s largest, most traditional financial markets to a broader pool of investors. RE observed, “Coinbase Ventures has concentrated much of its recent activity on teams moving established businesses and real-world assets onchain, a thesis that fits reinsurance neatly: premiums flow in, claims flow out, and a regulator watches the whole cycle. Re explained that its ecosystem brings together the onchain “re” protocol at re.xyz, operated by Resilience Foundation Cayman LLC, with the regulated reinsurance business under the “Cover Re” brand at coverre.com, operated by Cover Reinsurance SPC Ltd., a Cayman Islands Class B(iii) licensed exempted segregated portfolio company.

item.title: Wastewater Leak Under Texas Church Parking Lot Causes Millions in Damage
item.description: An old oil well sprang back to life under the parking lot of the First Baptist Church of Grandfalls in April. What will happen if the next leak isn’t under a parking lot, but a house or school? The state regulator, the Railroad Commission, spent $1.49 million plugging the leak and another $1.16 million disposing of the wastewater back underground.

item.title: People Moves: Sunstar Names Hearst as EVP, National Growth and Sales Strategy
item.description: Sunstar Insurance Group announced the appointment of Brad Hearst as executive vice president, national growth and sales strategy. In his role at Sunstar, Hearst will provide strategic direction for the firm’s national growth initiatives, encompassing producer development, sales strategy, performance management and practice group alignment. Hearst brings extensive insurance-industry leadership experience in driving organic growth, developing high-performing sales organizations and building disciplined sales processes that deliver measurable results across diverse markets.

item.title: Zurich Sees Data Center Boom Spurring Insurance Securitization
item.description: The level of investment into data centers globally may soon make it necessary for insurers involved to begin selling securitization products to spread the risk among a broader pool of investors, according to Zurich Insurance Group AG. Five years ago the average value of a data center project within Zurich’s portfolio was $150 million, whereas now it is $3 billion, Kinzer said. According to S&P Global, total insurable values for a single data center are expected to be as much as $30 billion per location, compared to $10 billion for some of the world’s biggest bridges.

item.title: Earnix launches AIOS to bring insurance-native AI orchestration to enterprise decision-making
item.description: The post Earnix launches AIOS to bring insurance-native AI orchestration to enterprise decision-making appeared first on Insurtech Insights . New AI Orchestration System combines AI agents, workflow automation, governance and human oversight to support underwriting, pricing, claims and customer engagement Earnix has unveiled AIOS, a new AI Orchestration System designed specifically for insurers, marking a significant expansion of the company’s intelligent decisioning capabilities across the insurance lifecycle. They must be built around dynamic intelligence that informs decisions as they are made.” AIOS combines decision orchestration, workflow automation, model management, governance controls and human-in-the-loop review capabilities to provide insurers with what Earnix describes as a governed operating model for scaling AI.

item.title: Corgi Insurance partners with Silicon Valley Bank to streamline insurance access for startups
item.description: The post Corgi Insurance partners with Silicon Valley Bank to streamline insurance access for startups appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Corgi Insurance and Silicon Valley Bank (SVB), a division of First Citizens Bank, have announced a strategic partnership aimed at helping startups and technology companies access commercial insurance coverage more quickly and efficiently. “Through our partnership with Corgi, we can connect clients with access to modern insurance solutions to optimize a client’s ability to manage risk, meet enterprise requirements, and focus on continuing to grow and scale.” Under the agreement, Silicon Valley Bank clients will gain access to commercial insurance products designed specifically for startups and growth-stage businesses operating in sectors including technology, artificial intelligence, software-as-a-service (SaaS), fintech and healthcare.

item.title: Insurance's Institutional Memory Crisis
item.description: The insurance profession is facing not merely a talent shortage but also a memory crisis. A retirement wave and cyclical amnesia are erasing institutional memory that could prevent billions in repeated mistakes. The insurance industry thought it understood hurricane risk in Florida.

item.title: A Risk in Roadside Assistance Plans
item.description: For insurance carriers whose policyholder benefits include roadside assistance, this creates a category of vendor risk that rarely appears in actuarial models. Before committing to a multi-year roadside assistance agreement, risk managers should require answers to the following: As roadside assistance companies consolidate, carriers must evaluate vendor solvency alongside traditional metrics like coverage and service levels.

item.title: CRC Specialty launches Insurisk middle market property program
item.description: CRC Group has launched a Middle Market Property Program through its exclusive Insurisk platform, targeting non-catastrophe commercial property risks for single-location and small schedule accounts. This program provides brokers with a flexible and efficient solution for middle market property risks while maintaining a strong underwriting approach across the portfolio.” – Insurisk President Alex Bonds. “Insurisk Middle Market Property reflects our continued commitment to delivering specialized underwriting solutions backed by responsive capacity and disciplined risk selection.

item.title: Inszone acquires Webtec Insurance Services
item.description: Inszone Insurance has acquired Webtec Insurance Services, a personal lines agency specializing in auto and homeowners coverage, the Sacramento-based brokerage said Thursday. Webtec was founded more than 40 years ago in California and later moved its base to Colorado, though California remains the bulk of its book. The acquisition extends Inszone’s personal lines footprint in California and Colorado.

item.title: World Insurance Associates acquires Wolpert Insurance Agency
item.description: World Insurance Associates has acquired Wolpert Insurance Agency, a Worcester, Massachusetts-based broker specializing in transportation coverage, the Iselin, New Jersey firm said. Founded in 1956, Wolpert serves businesses and families across Massachusetts and Rhode Island, with a niche in trucking, limousine, and bobtail insurance. Principals Mike Wolpert and Robert Mucci will continue with the firm.

item.title: Major Hack Campaign Against Fortinet Devices Compromised Prominent Organizations
item.description: The firm said that some 75,000 Fortinet firewall and VPN devices—tools that companies use to protect their networks and allow employees to log in remotely—had been compromised, potentially allowing the hackers to penetrate deeper into these organizations and steal data. Researchers say a sweeping hacking campaign targeting devices made by Fortinet has led to compromises across the internet, with evidence of password theft at Fortune 500 companies and government agencies in more than 15 countries. Hudson Rock described the scale of the spy campaign as “staggering.” Related: Hacking Group Claims Major Hack of Novo Nordisk And Attempted Extortion “The scale of this breach touches nearly every sector of the global economy, sparing no industry,” it said in a blog post published on Wednesday.

item.title: 5 Principles for Insurers: Testing Agentic AI’s Next Wave
item.description: As insurance organizations evaluate and experiment with technologies and the next wave of agentic AI systems, these five principles can guide their approaches. For insurers, the instinctive response was understandable: The technology feels too dangerous, too immature and too loosely governed for real-world deployment. OpenClaw’s significance isn’t that insurers should immediately implement it.

item.title: RSUI CEO McCrorie to Retire in March 2027
item.description: Specialty insurer RSUI announced that Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Phillip McCrorie will step down as CEO at year-end 2026, concluding a career spanning nearly 40 years in the insurance industry. He will remain RSUI’s executive chairman through the first quarter of 2027 to ensure a seamless leadership transition. McCrorie joined RSUI in 2003 as chief financial officer.

item.title: The Boardroom Blind Spot: When Success Hides Disruption
item.description: Disruption rarely announces itself during a crisis. It often appears when the business still looks strong. Boards often become more aggressive only when performance weakens.

item.title: AT&T Ventures’ Head Vikram Taneja On The New Rules of Seed-Stage Defensibility
item.description: In his role as head of AT&T Ventures , Vikram Taneja leads the corporate venture capital arm of the telecommunications giant, managing the corporation’s portfolio across direct equity investments, warrants and limited-partner fund positions. Vikram Taneja: The old definition of technical risk was “can they build it?” Although not entirely absent at the seed stage, I’d say it is becoming less relevant given the dramatically lower barrier to building software with AI tools. Taneja also served as a director at Orix Ventures , where he focused on growth-capital debt and equity investments in mid- to late-stage technology businesses, as well as holding corporate finance and investment banking roles at J.P.

item.title: Cat bonds played significant price discovery role at Florida renewal: Schwebach, Gallagher Re
item.description: The catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) markets played a very significant role in determining price levels and driving improved terms and conditions for reinsurance buyers at the June 2026 Florida reinsurance renewals, according to Gallagher Re’s Head of North American Property, Adam Schwebach. “Cat bonds played a very significant role in the price levels for this year, and that’s a market that continues to play a larger role in the overall risk transfer strategies in Florida. “Terms and conditions continued to improve for clients, given oversubscriptions, and both the ILS and traditional markets played a massive role in this,” Schwebach explained.

item.title: Solidum to shift cat bond portfolio closer to index for hurricane season, but selectivity key
item.description: With the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season now underway, the key meteorological factors influencing tropical cyclones have pointed towards a below-average season in terms of storm numbers, which affects the tactical positioning of an insurance-linked securities (ILS) or catastrophe bond portfolio, a new report from Solidum Partners AG has highlighted. Solidum noted that the 2026 hurricane season presents a different macro setup than the prior two years, with a number of key themes that drive the firm’s outlook and positioning for this year’s season: Firstly, the ILS investment manager observes that El Niño dominates the outlook, with forecasts at the time from NOAA/CPC calling for a 61% probability of El Niño emerging in May–July 2026, with 88–94% persistence into Q4. Last year’s Atlantic hurricane season was particularly unique as it marked for the first time in a decade that no hurricane made landfall in the continental United States, despite three category 5 storms forming in the Atlantic basin.

item.title: After May, Allstate’s cat losses reach $1.16bn pre-tax for 2026 aggregate risk period
item.description: Allstate begun the new annual aggregate risk period, that its Sanders Re program catastrophe bonds and excess of loss reinsurance are subject to, with $870 million of pre-tax catastrophe losses in April 2026 . As a reminder, the risk period for Allstate’s annual aggregate catastrophe bonds under its Sanders Re program, which sit in the nationwide aggregate tower, begins on April 1st each year. Consequently, Allstate’s $289 million in pre-tax catastrophe losses ($228 million after-tax) for May 2026 is well below the recent average.

item.title: Insurer innovation could lead to faster DB risk transfer wind-up: Hymans Robertson
item.description: Hymans Robertson, a UK-based pensions and financial services consultancy, said it expects increased innovation from insurers to lead to the faster wind up of DB pensions schemes following risk transfer transactions. In a recent paper, Hymans Robertson argues that insurer-led data and GMP work, investment in digital capabilities and improved processes are shortening the post-transaction phase of DB scheme buy-out journeys, with the potential to help address delays in winding up schemes. Hymans Robertson said approximately 75% of wind-up projects experienced delays, leading to uncertainty and increased costs for sponsors.

item.title: MSIG USA names Phillip Rhodes as Head of Global Insurance Programs
item.description: MSIG USA, the US insurance arm of global insurer MS&AD Insurance Group Holdings, has appointed Phillip Rhodes as Head of Global Insurance Programs. In his new role, Rhodes will lead the insurer’s global insurance program strategy and development efforts, enhancing the solutions and service for clients with international operations. Brian Botkin, Chief Underwriting Officer, MSIG USA, commented on the appointment, “Phil’s deep experience in multinational insurance, combined with his understanding of global networks and cross-border program management, makes him an outstanding addition to our team.

item.title: Allstate sees May cat losses ease to $289m after active April
item.description: The Allstate Corporation has estimated catastrophe losses of $289 million pre-tax ($228 million after-tax) for May 2026, bringing its combined catastrophe losses for April and May to $1.16 billion pre-tax ($915 million after-tax). The May 2026 catastrophe losses represent a significant slowdown from April, which came in at $870 million ($687 million after-tax) and included 10 wind and hail events . Catastrophe losses for last month also mark a large decrease from May 2025, which were $777 million ($614 million after-tax) .

item.title: World Insurance Adds Wolpert Agency of Massachusetts
item.description: Global insurance broker World Insurance Associates reported that it recently bought the business of Wolpert Insurance Agency of Worcester, Massachusetts. It also sells personal and commercial lines insurance throughout Massachusetts and Rhode Island. Mike Wolpert and Robert Mucci are principals of the agency.

item.title: People Moves: Fortegra, Alabama DOI Put New Faces in Key Roles
item.description: Meanwhile, the Alabama Department of Insurance has completed a restructuring of the department, with new faces in key positions, including new deputy commissioners. Fortegra Group, the Jacksonville-based parent company of specialty insurers, named Anthony Katz, a longtime actuary, as senior vice president for reserving. Katz will lead Fortegra’s team of actuaries, overseeing reserving, credit insurance, statistical reporting and compliance with the new IFRS 17 accounting standard, the company said.

item.title: California Homeowners Insurance Costs Still 41% Below National Average, Report Shows
item.description: California homeowners pay 41% below the national average for homeowners insurance, a study from LendingTree shows. LendingTree’s State of Home Insurance: 2026 report , which is based on rates publicly sourced from insurer filings, shows California homeowners paid an average of $1,413 annually for home insurance compared with the national average of $2,395. However, homeowners insurance rates in California have risen 53.7% since 2020, the report shows.

item.title: PwC and Vitesse partner to modernise insurance fund management
item.description: Vitesse and PwC have joined forces to deliver a new proposition for the delegated authority insurance market, aimed at helping insurers, MGAs and TPAs address longstanding challenges around loss fund management, cash visibility and operational efficiency. PwC and Vitesse said they are continuing to work with insurers and delegated authority market participants that are evaluating how digital treasury solutions can support broader transformation initiatives, improve operational efficiency and deliver greater control over insurance fund management. The collaboration combines Vitesse’s digital payments and treasury management technology with PwC’s expertise in insurance operations, finance transformation and change management.

item.title: Corgi partners with Silicon Valley Bank
item.description: Corgi and Silicon Valley Bank, a division of First Citizens Bank, announced a partnership that will provide SVB clients with “streamlined access” to Corgi’s digital insurance products and risk management solutions. Through our partnership with Corgi, we can connect clients with access to modern insurance solutions to optimize a client’s ability to manage risk, meet enterprise requirements, and focus on continuing to grow and scale.” – Nick Christian, Head of National Fintech and Specialty Finance with Silicon Valley Bank. Silicon Valley Bank has spent decades supporting the world’s most ambitious companies, and we’re excited to work together to make modern insurance more accessible to the next generation of builders.” – Nico Laqua, Founder and CEO of Corgi Insurance.

item.title: AXA XL partners with Samsara
item.description: AXA XL announced a partnership with fleet management and safety platform Samsara. As part of the partnership, Samsara’s platform will be incorporated into AXA XL’s suite of risk solutions in the UK. “Partnering with Samsara aligns perfectly with our vision to harness technology for better risk understanding and management, and we have seen its impact in reducing claims frequency and costs by 10-20%.

item.title: Connie Health buys Clearlink’s Medicare business
item.description: Connie Health has completed its acquisition of Clearlink’s Medicare business and closed a $40 million Series B round to fund further M&A in Medicare distribution. “The infrastructure we’ve built makes Clearlink’s scale a perfect fit for Connie Health. Clearlink is the AI-native Medicare navigation platform’s tenth acquisition.

item.title: El Observatorio de Catástrofes se integrará en Unespa en 2028
item.description: Unespa y la Asociación Cluster Catástrofes han suscrito un convenio de colaboración que garantiza la continuidad del Observatorio de Catástrofes, con vistas a su integración definitiva en la estructura de la asociación a partir de 2028. El acuerdo fue firmado por los presidentes de ambas entidades, Mirenchu del Valle y Pedro Tomey, respectivamente.

item.title: Mutual Médica aumenta un 6,2% sus primas y supera los 100.000 mutualistas en 2025
item.description: Mutual Médica ha cerrado el ejercicio 2025 con un volumen de primas de 178,9 millones de euros, un 6,2% más respecto al año anterior.

item.title: Meridiano presenta sus líneas estratégicas para los próximos años
item.description: Meridiano Seguros ha celebrado en Granada su primera Convención Comercial Nacional en la que, coincidiendo con su 60 aniversario, ha reunido a más de 150 profesionales para reforzar la conexión entre los equipos, fomentar la colaboración entre territorios y trasladar su visión de futuro.

item.title: Can AI Help Coastal Cities Prepare for Rising Seas And Extreme Events?
item.description: Sea levels are rising, and with them, the risks posed by extreme coastal events, such as storm surges – temporary rises in sea level caused mainly by storms, which are among the primary drivers of coastal flooding. For the more than 10% of the global population living in low-lying coastal regions, the combination of gradual mean sea level rise and increasingly intense extreme events represents a growing threat. Related: Zurich Insurance Expands Data-Center Offering Beyond the US For coastal planners and policymakers, the key issue is not just the expected rise in mean sea level, but the changes in the likelihood and severity of extreme events.

item.title: NTSB: North Dakota Derailment Proves the Need to Replace Flawed Tank Cars
item.description: A fiery North Dakota derailment that happened two years ago demonstrated yet again why the National Transportation Safety Board has been urging the rail industry for decades to replace flawed tank cars that tend to rupture in a crash. The NTSB said in its final report last week that the workhorse DOT-111 tank cars are long overdue for replacement because of the way the devastating impact of a derailment is magnified anytime hazardous chemicals leak, especially if they catch fire as they did in this derailment just outside the town of Bordulac, North Dakota. The derailment itself was caused by a culvert that collapsed under the train, breaking a rail, but NTSB said the leak-prone tank cars made the resulting crash so much worse.

item.title: Asia Reports Sharp Rise in Cybercrimes and Scams, Interpol Says
item.description: Illegal cyber activities accounted for around a third of all crimes recorded in some Asian countries, with scams the most widespread and financially damaging, according to a new Interpol report. Related: Hacking Group Claims Major Hack of Novo Nordisk And Attempted Extortion Of the 18 Interpol member states in Asia and South Pacific that responded to a survey, more than half reported that cybercrime made up 30% of all crimes recorded nationally. “The findings in this report highlight a rapidly evolving cyber threat landscape across Asia and the South Pacific, where cybercriminals are leveraging artificial intelligence, ransomware-as-a-service models and sophisticated social engineering techniques on an industrial scale,” Neal Jetton, who oversees the Cybercrime Directorate at Interpol in Singapore, said in a statement.

item.title: Crawford appoints duo as loss adjusters in Singapore
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles
